---
title: "Mermaid Architecture"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Arhitecture Overview

```{mermaid}
graph TD
    subgraph "External World"
        Galaxy[Galaxy/IRIDA History]
        API["API eg. BioBlend, .csv"]
    end


    subgraph "The Dashboard (Shiny for Python)"
        direction TB
        
        %% TODO- need to integrate relations with config layer
        subgraph "Ingress & Prep"
            direction TB 
            
            Connector["
            <b><u>Connection Layer</u></b>
            CONNECTOR: 
            fetches raw data"]
            
            Ingest["
            <b><u>Ingestion Layer</u></b>
            INGRESS: 
            reads files
            validates schema
            ensures data integrity"]

            Wrangler["
            <b><u>Data Wrangling Layer</u></b>
            TRANSFORMER:
            Converts raw data to 
            TIDY data contract"]
        end

        subgraph "Configuration Layer (Metadata Registry)"
            DataRules["<b><u>Configuration Layer</u></b><br/>species_config.yaml"]
            UIPrefs["<b>UI Preferences</b><br/>(user_prefs.json)"]
            SystemEnv["<b>Environment Config</b><br/>(secrets.env / paths.yaml)"]
        end


        subgraph "The Orchestrator"
            Logic{{"<b>App Logic</b><br/>(app.py Server)"}}
        end

        subgraph "Presentation"
            Factory[<b><u>Visualization Factory</u></b><br/>viz_core.py]
            UI[<b><u>Display & Interaction</u></b><br/>app.py UI]
        end
    
    end

    %% Data Flow
    Galaxy -->|Raw Files| API
    API -->|Auth/Stream| Connector
    Connector --> Ingest
    Ingest -->|Validated Data| Wrangler
    DataRules --> Ingest
    Wrangler -->|Tidy Data Contract| Logic
    
    DataRules -.->|Species Metadata| Logic
    Logic -->|Filtered Data| Factory
    Factory -->|Plotly Object| UI
    UI -->|User Inputs| Logic
```

| Term | Technical ID | Responsibility (The "Why") | Analogy |
|--------------|--------------|------------------------------|--------------|
| Connection to External World | connector.py | Fetches raw data via API (BioBlend). | The Courier. |
| Ingestion Layer | ingress.py | Reads files and validates schema. | The Doorman. |
| Data Wrangling | transformer.py | Converts raw data to Tidy Data Contract. | The Prep Cook. |
| Configuration Layer | config.yaml | Stores species-specific metadata mapping. | The Rulebook. |
| Orchestrator | app.py (Server) | Coordinates data flow and user input. | The Conductor. |
| Visualization Factory | viz_core.py | Data-agnostic Plotly function library. | The Printing Press. |
| Display & Interaction | app.py (UI) | Layout, Sliders, and Theme. | The Shop Window. |

| Your Term | Architectural Term | Responsibility |
|------------------------|------------------------|------------------------|
| Connection to External World | Data Connector (BioBlend/API) | The "Bridge." It handles authentication and fetches raw data. |
| Ingestion Layer | Data Ingress | The "Gatekeeper." It reads CSV/JSON/VCF and ensures the file isn't corrupted. |
| Data Wrangling | Transformer (Tidy Layer) | The "Cleaner." Converts raw bioinfo outputs into a Tidy Data Contract (one row per observation). |
| Configuration Layer | Metadata Registry | The "Brain." A JSON/YAML file that tells the dashboard: "For this species, look at column X for serotype." |
| Orchestrator Logic | The Controller | The "Manager." In Shiny, this is your server logic that reacts to user input and calls the right functions. |
| Visualization Factory | Plotting Library (Agnostic) | The "Artist." Functions that take a dataframe and return a Plotly object. They don't care if the data is Salmonella or Listeria. |
| Display & Interaction | Presentation Layer (UI) | The "Face." The HTML/CSS layout and the sliders/dropdowns the user clicks. |
